title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
logline: A renowned cardiac surgeon's life unravels when a mysterious teenage boy he's befriended exacts a supernatural revenge that forces him to make an impossible sacrifice.
antagonistOrConflict: Martin, a teenage boy enacting a metaphysical curse that paralyzes Steven's family

actBreaks.description: Bob's mysterious paralysis becomes undeniable and irreversible, launching the family into medical crisis and Steven into desperate investigation.

validation.ruling: Scene #42 cannot be Act 1 break as it shows doctors reassuring Steven that Bob's tests are normal—this is false security, not a turning point. Scene #25 (Martin's house visit at 31%) is the true act break where Martin explicitly threatens Steven's family, revealing the supernatural curse and fundamentally changing the story from mysterious friendship to life-or-death stakes.

actBreaks.description: Steven kidnaps and shoots Martin, attempting to break the curse through violence, but the situation escalates as Anna decides they must sacrifice one child.

validation.ruling: Scene #86 reveals Martin imprisoned and bloodied in Steven's basement, marking the irreversible shift from victim to captor. This fundamental role reversal at 83.3% forces Steven into the final act's moral abyss, where he must choose between killing Martin or sacrificing his family—the story cannot return to its previous state.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a slow-burn descent into nightmare logic. Act One (scenes 1-42) establishes Steven's controlled world through repetitive rituals—hospital routines, family dinners, secret meetings with Martin at Burger King—before the supernatural intrusion shatters everything. The pacing is deliberately methodical, almost clinical, mirroring Steven's profession. Act Two (scenes 43-86) is the longest section, chronicling the family's medical deterioration with documentary-like precision as both children lose mobility, refuse food, and begin bleeding from the eyes. The script refuses conventional escalation; instead, it accumulates dread through repetition and the characters' increasingly desperate attempts to rationalize the irrational. Act Three (scenes 87-113) compresses into a claustrophobic endgame where Steven must choose which child to kill, culminating in the chilling final scene at Burger King where the family sits in silence, waiting for Martin's judgment.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains an unnervingly flat affect throughout its 113 scenes, averaging just over one page per scene. Early scenes (1-30) establish rhythm through parallel structure—hospital/home/Burger King meetings. The middle section (scenes 42-75) slows dramatically as the children's condition worsens, with multiple hospital scenes creating a sense of medical futility. The final act accelerates through shorter, more fragmented scenes (80-113), particularly the basement confrontations and the family's frantic search for Kim. The script's refusal to provide catharsis or explanation creates mounting tension despite the measured pacing.

incitingIncident.description: Steven waits at a Burger King where Martin, a teenage boy, arrives late and apologizes before going to get food, offering to buy Steven chicken wings.

validation.ruling: Scene #4 introduces Martin, the mysterious teenage boy who will become the antagonist, establishing the strange relationship with Steven that drives the entire narrative. This meeting shatters Steven's ordinary world and sets the main conflict in motion, occurring at the ideal 5.6% position.

midpoint.description: Martin gives Steven a Swiss army knife as a present in the hospital canteen, then Steven accuses Martin of killing his father and has security escort him out.

validation.ruling: Scene #47 is a confrontational dialogue but lacks the irreversible shift required for a midpoint. Scene #40 (Bob's sudden collapse at 47%) is the true point of no return—the mysterious curse Martin warned about becomes undeniably real, forcing Steven from denial into desperate action. This medical crisis raises stakes dramatically and transforms Steven's approach from passive dismissal to active crisis management.

climax.description: Steven ties and gags all three family members positioned in a triangle around him, puts on a balaclava to blind himself, raises a rifle, spins, and fires one shot.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #112 is the ultimate confrontation where Steven, blindfolded and spinning with a rifle, randomly shoots his own family to satisfy Martin's demand for a child's death. This is the peak dramatic tension, the final unavoidable choice, and resolves the central story question of whether Steven will sacrifice his family, occurring at the perfect 100% position.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - OPERATING THEATRE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven performs open heart surgery with Matthew and surgical nurses in precise synchronicity, followed by opening credits.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - SURGERY PREPARATION ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven and Matthew wash their hands after surgery and Steven examines Matthew's watch, commenting on its water resistance and suggesting he would prefer a metal strap over leather.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - STEVEN’S OFFICE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Steven unlocks his office, changes from his white coat into a blazer, and picks up his briefcase.

scenes.heading: INT. BURGER KING - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Steven waits at a Burger King where Martin, a teenage boy, arrives late and apologizes before going to get food, offering to buy Steven chicken wings.

scenes.heading: EXT. SEASIDE ROAD - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Steven and Martin stand by the car near the sea, and Martin apologizes for being late, explaining he was riding a friend's new motorbike.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - GARDEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven sits in his garden with his wife Anna and children Kim and Bob, discussing whether Kim can attend a friend's birthday party.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anna removes her makeup while Steven lies in bed, and they discuss a black dress she bought that he liked.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - STEVEN’S OFFICE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven examines a patient when his secretary interrupts to tell him a boy is waiting outside urgently.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven confronts Martin in the hospital corridor about visiting without calling first, but softens when Martin shows him the new leather watch strap he bought.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KIM’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven watches Kim perform vocal exercises in her bedroom while Bob combs his hair, then Steven praises Kim's improved breathing technique.

scenes.heading: INT. CARDIOLOGY CONFERENCE - HALL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven gives a speech at a cardiology conference, making a joke about Dr. Gruentzig's death that draws scattered laughter.

scenes.heading: INT. CARDIOLOGY CONFERENCE - PARTY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven talks with Anna, Matthew, and an elderly man at the conference party, where Anna mentions Steven hasn't had a drink in three years due to elevated liver enzymes.

scenes.heading: EXT. SEASIDE ROAD - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Steven eats ice cream with Martin by the sea and notices Martin isn't wearing his watch, then asks about Martin's friends and father.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Martin arrives at Steven's house carrying flowers and a plastic bag, and Anna greets him at the door.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - GARDEN - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Martin meets Steven's family in the garden and gives Bob and Kim small gifts from his bag.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KIM’S BEDROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Martin sits in Kim's bedroom with Bob, asks about Kim's choir experience, reveals he has armpit hair and Kim has gotten her first period, then requests Kim sing for him.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Martin and Kim walk along a road with awkward distance between them until Kim catches up, undoes her ponytail, and shakes out her hair.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Martin sits on a park bench watching Kim sing a song while she sways and holds a lit cigarette.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Martin drinks homemade lemonade with Steven's family in the living room and suggests Bob install a pull-up bar in his doorway.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - NIGHT.
scenes.summary: Anna flosses her teeth while Steven lies in bed, and they discuss Martin being charming and how his father died in a car crash before Steven's phone rings.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven steps into the hallway to take Martin's call, where Martin invites him to dinner at his house the next night with his mother.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - SURGERY PREP ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven and Matthew wash up after surgery, and Matthew invites Steven and Anna to lunch on Saturday for barbecued steelhead.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL CAR PARK - EVENING
scenes.summary: Steven drives out of the hospital car park and sees a figure in Martin's jacket run toward the fire escape and disappear.

scenes.heading: INT. MARTIN’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven has dinner with Martin and his mother, and when Steven asks if Martin came to the hospital that day, Martin denies it.

scenes.heading: INT. MARTIN’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven watches a film with Martin's mother after Martin goes to bed, and she tells Steven that Martin loves him and compliments his hands before performing oral sex on him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Steven crosses a major ethical boundary by engaging sexually with Martin's mother, fundamentally altering his relationship with Martin and introducing a secret that will complicate the narrative.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - STEVEN’S OFFICE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven finds Martin waiting in his office, and Martin removes his shirt claiming his heart aches and he's worried because heart problems are hereditary.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - STRESS TEST ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Martin runs on a treadmill with electrodes attached while Steven measures his blood pressure during a stress test.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - STEVEN’S OFFICE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Martin removes the electrodes and asks Steven to take off his shirt to compare body hair, which Steven eventually does, then Martin asks Steven to kiss him.

scenes.heading: INT. MATTHEW’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Steven watches Matthew gut a steelhead trout in the kitchen while drinking ginger beer.

scenes.heading: INT. BURGER KING - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Martin sits alone at Burger King after eating, checks his watch, and calls someone who is late, pleading with them to come even briefly.

scenes.heading: EXT. MATTHEW’S HOUSE - GARDEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Matthew grills trout in his garden while Anna helps Mary set the table and Steven sits looking ill at ease, holding wine but not drinking.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven sits on the sofa eating a sandwich and feeding ham to the dog when Kim arrives home and tells him she saw Martin today.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Martin stands across from Steven's house holding a helmet beside his friend's red motorbike, watching the house with a blank expression as the living room light goes off.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven, Anna, and Kim have breakfast without Bob, and Anna coaches Kim on how to answer phones at her eye clinic.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BOB’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven enters Bob's bedroom to find his son pale and frightened, claiming his legs are numb and he cannot move them.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bob's sudden paralysis marks the beginning of the curse's manifestation, launching the central crisis that will drive the remainder of the narrative.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - GARAGE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven lifts Bob into the car in the garage while Anna helps, then Anna gets into the driver's seat.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL ENTRANCE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven lifts Bob out of the car at the hospital entrance and practically carries him inside with Anna following.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - NEUROLOGIST’S OFFICE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Dr. Nichols examines Bob while Steven watches and tries to reassure his son with jokes about unwashed feet.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven tells Anna in the corridor that Bob passed his neurological examination and is fine, dismissing Anna's suggestion of an MRI.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ENTRANCE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Bob collapses to the ground the moment he and Anna step outside the hospital, and nurses rush over to lift him into a wheelchair.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - MRI SCANNER ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Bob lies inside an MRI machine while Steven, Anna, and Dr. Nichols watch from behind a glass window.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - PATIENT’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dr. Nichols reports that all of Bob's tests came back clear, and recommends keeping Bob in the hospital another day for observation while suggesting psychological stress as a possible cause.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Martin rides his friend's motorbike without a helmet while Kim sits behind him holding on tight with tears streaming down her face.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - GARDEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anna waters plants in the garden when Kim arrives home, and they briefly discuss Bob being kept in the hospital as a precaution.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven and Anna walk down the hospital corridor carrying doughnuts, discussing whether Bob got up or went to the toilet.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - PATIENT’S ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven and Anna enter Bob's room to find Martin already there visiting, and Martin mentions Bob wet himself because he refused help going to the toilet.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL CANTEEN - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Martin gives Steven a Swiss army knife as a present in the hospital canteen, then Steven accuses Martin of killing his father and has security escort him out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Steven directly confronts Martin with the accusation of killing his father, rupturing their relationship and forcing the underlying conflict into the open in an irreversible confrontation.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ENTRANCE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Two security guards escort Martin out of the hospital building without resistance as he glances back over his shoulder.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - PATIENT’S ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Anna reads to Bob in his hospital room while Steven enters restlessly and tries to get Bob to eat a doughnut, but Bob refuses.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - DIRECTOR’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven, Anna, Dr. Nichols, the Hospital Director, and other doctors review Bob's test results, with Anna reporting Bob has stopped eating and keeps vomiting while Steven insists tests need to be redone.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BATHROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kim applies lipstick, brushes her hair, and sprays herself with perfume in the bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KIM’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kim and Martin kiss on her bed, she strips to her underwear and lies down, and Martin compliments her appearance.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anna marches angrily down the hospital corridor with Steven following; he apologizes and insists on staying to rerun all of Bob's tests while she goes home to Kim.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven pushes a groggy, half-asleep Bob in a wheelchair manically up and down hospital corridors.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - BLOOD TEST ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven takes blood from Bob and gives the sample to a nurse.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - MRI SCANNER ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bob lies inside an MRI machine as Steven watches him disappear into it.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - ULTRASOUND ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven stands next to a doctor who performs an abdominal ultrasound on Bob.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven pushes Bob back in the wheelchair, then lifts him and walks him down the corridor before letting him fall to the floor; he kneels and proposes they play a secret-telling game.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S OFFICE - HOSPITAL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Anna finds Steven asleep at his desk and brings him coffee and a clean shirt; he has no test results to report and suggests the paralysis might be psychological.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL - REHEARSAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Kim collapses while singing in the school choir; the music stops as everyone falls silent and the choirmaster rushes to her.
scenes.turningPointNote: The curse spreads to Kim — the second child is now paralyzed, escalating the family crisis.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Anna sits outside Bob's hospital room crying while Matthew hugs her, and a nurse exits with a food trolley.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - PATIENT’S ROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Steven feeds Kim pieces of fruit in the hospital room where both children lie in adjacent beds; Kim spits out the fruit and chokes when Steven insists she eat.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - PATIENT’S ROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: A nurse inserts nasogastric feeding tubes into both children; Steven notices a new MP3 player on Kim's bedside table and learns Martin gave it to her.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven drives to Martin's house, rings the doorbell frantically, bangs on the door threatening to break it down and sexually assault Martin and his mother, then tries to break in through the balcony doors.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL GROUNDS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven confesses to Anna outside the hospital that he's been meeting Martin for six months at a diner; Anna asks if he'd been drinking the day Martin's father died, and Steven admits the patient had an arrhythmia and stroke.
scenes.turningPointNote: Steven finally reveals his relationship with Martin and the circumstances of the father's death to Anna, bringing the hidden truth into their marriage.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE- BATHROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Steven takes a shower.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Steven sits naked on the edge of his bed with a vacant, blank gaze in the dark room, then stands and hurriedly gets dressed.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - FRONT DOOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Steven finds an envelope on his doormat containing the watch he gave Martin with one strap snapped off and a note reading that time shows no mercy and metal straps are better than leather.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - PATIENT’S ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Anna sits with the children in the hospital room; Kim asks if Martin came by while she was sleeping, and Anna turns her onto her side so they cannot make eye contact.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Anna tells Steven she saw Kim's legs move when Martin's name was mentioned; Steven dismisses it as coincidence and exhaustion, insisting it means Kim is getting better, and sends Anna home to rest.

scenes.heading: EXT. ANNA’S CAR - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Anna drives with contradicting thoughts going through her mind.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARTIN’S HOUSE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Anna pulls over outside Martin's house, looks to see if anyone is home, takes a deep breath, and walks up to the front door.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARTIN’S HOUSE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Martin opens the door in a T-shirt and boxer shorts holding spaghetti, recognizes Anna, and invites her inside.

scenes.heading: INT. MARTIN’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Anna tells Martin that Steven told her about his father; Martin puts down his spaghetti, smiles faintly, and says Steven killed his father and then began an affair with his mother.
scenes.turningPointNote: Martin reveals to Anna that Steven has been having an affair with Martin's mother, adding a devastating layer to the betrayal.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - MEETING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A medical council meeting takes place with Anna, Steven, Matthew, the Hospital Director, Dr. Nichols, and visiting consultants; Anna sits defeated with her arms folded, watching silently through a window.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL ENTRANCE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Hospital Director says goodbye to the visiting consultants and tells Steven there's no reason for the children to stay in hospital; Steven angrily responds that if he ran the hospital he'd feel like a complete failure.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anna marches angrily down the corridor with Steven following; she suddenly falls over, then gets up claiming she tripped on her shoelace and continues walking almost at a run.
scenes.turningPointNote: Anna falls, suggesting the curse has now spread to her — the entire family is now affected.

scenes.heading: INT. CAFETERIA - MORNING
scenes.summary: Anna meets Matthew at a cafeteria and asks if he was the anaesthetist for Jonathan Lang four years ago; Matthew unconvincingly denies remembering and claims Steven was a great surgeon.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESERTED ROAD - MORNING
scenes.summary: Anna manually stimulates Matthew to ejaculation in his parked car on a deserted road, then he immediately starts the engine and drives off.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - FRONT DOOR - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Paramedics carry the children into Steven's house through the open front door while Steven manically supervises the procedure.

scenes.heading: INT. KIM’S BEDROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Anna changes the feeding solution in Bob and Kim's Levin tubes in Kim's rearranged bedroom where both children's beds have been placed.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven eats dinner while Anna silently toys with her food; he fills her wine glass and tries to engage her in conversation about taking the children to the beach house, but she remains stubbornly silent and stares at him.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven sets the alarm clock and tells Anna there's no need to argue; she lies down with her back to him and says they should wait until they're all dead, then he'll see there's nothing to worry about.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven stands alone in the living room smoking, sits in an armchair, then stands again and quietly takes his car keys and coat before shutting the door.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven enters the bedroom where Anna lies awake, sits on the bed, takes her hand, and asks her to get up and come with him.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven unlocks the basement door and shows Anna that Martin is chained to a pipe in the corner with his feet and hands bound, his face bloodied and swollen; a rifle sits on the table.
scenes.turningPointNote: Steven has kidnapped and beaten Martin, escalating from desperation to violent action and crossing a moral threshold.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - GARDEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Anna scrubs blood from the back seat of Steven's car in the garden while the dog watches; a muffled gunshot is heard and the dog starts barking.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Anna opens the basement door to find Steven has shot Martin in the leg; Steven threatens to kill him, but Martin asks what will happen then and says Steven won't be able to explain how his family died.
scenes.turningPointNote: Steven shoots Martin but realizes killing him won't break the curse — the family will still die and he'll have no explanation, trapping him in an impossible situation.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KIM’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Kim sits in a wheelchair smoking and tells Bob that Steven didn't kill Martin; she says it would have been like killing four people with one shot and announces she's going to live with Martin.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KIM’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Bob drags himself over to the desk, struggles to open the drawer, and takes out a pair of scissors.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Bob drags himself into the kitchen with his face and hands covered in hair after cutting his own hair; he apologizes to Steven for not getting a haircut right away and says he doesn't know what he was thinking.

scenes.heading: INT. - STEVEN’S HOUSE - KIM’S BEDROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Steven lays Bob in his bed, tucks him in, and strokes his hair.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - GARDEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven huddles behind flowers in the garden sobbing, then pulls himself together, wipes his eyes, walks to the house, closes the French windows, and switches off the living room light.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL - CORRIDOR - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven waits outside the Headmaster's office, then the secretary ushers him inside.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL - HEADMASTER’S OFFICE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven signs papers in the Headmaster's office and lies that the children are walking slowly and eating properly, informing the Headmaster they'll need to be off school a little while longer.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Anna brings Bob and Kim down to the basement where she washes Martin's wound, kisses both his bare feet, and allows Kim to kiss Martin on the lips while Bob watches from a distance.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anna emerges from the bathroom in her bathrobe, lies next to Steven revealing parts of her naked body, and tells him not to kill her but that the logical choice is to kill a child because they can have another.
scenes.turningPointNote: Anna explicitly proposes that Steven kill one of their children as the solution, bringing the family to the threshold of an unthinkable decision.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kim drags herself into the basement and tells Martin they can escape together on his friend's motorbike if he makes her well first, but Martin doesn't respond.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A noise wakes Steven; he turns on the light, gets out of bed, and rushes out of the bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KIM’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven enters Kim's bedroom to find her bed empty with her feeding tube removed and her mobile phone on the desk; Bob is asleep in the other bed.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven and Anna search the house and grounds for Kim but do not find her.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BASEMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven finds the basement door open, goes down, and hits Martin demanding to know where Kim is; Martin claims he didn't touch her and doesn't know where she is.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anna drives slowly while Steven walks ahead scanning the road with headlights until they find Kim dragging herself along the pavement with bloody hands and knees trying to cross the road.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven treats Kim's wounds while Anna assists, and Kim apologizes for running away and begs her father to kill her right there so she can atone for his sins.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kim volunteers herself as the sacrifice, offering to die to save the family and end the curse.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KITCHEN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven returns from the hospital with feeding formula and discovers Anna has released Martin from the basement; Steven hits Anna and demands to know why she let him go.
scenes.turningPointNote: Anna releases Martin, eliminating Steven's leverage and forcing him toward the final choice.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - CHILDREN’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Anna bathes Bob's limbs while Kim sits in her wheelchair practicing clinic phone scripts her mother taught her.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Steven sits on the bed staring at the wardrobe, then stands over his sleeping wife contemplating whether to kill her to save the children before stepping back.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - CHILDREN’S BEDROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Bob's eyes start bleeding onto his pillow while listening to music, and Kim calls for their father saying Bob is dying.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bob reaches the final stage of the curse with bleeding eyes, signaling imminent death and forcing Steven's hand.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - CHILDREN’S BEDROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Steven wipes blood from Bob's face and changes his pillowcase while Bob asks about Steven's best friend Matthew and reveals Matthew used to call their mother frequently.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - KIM’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven undresses Kim and dresses her in the outfit she had chosen for Claire's party while she avoids his gaze; Bob sits in his wheelchair watching them.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven tells Anna that Bob is bleeding and to come to the living room now; Anna asks about the children and says she'll wear a black dress before Steven leaves.

scenes.heading: INT. STEVEN’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Steven ties and gags all three family members positioned in a triangle around him, puts on a balaclava to blind himself, raises a rifle, spins, and fires one shot.
scenes.turningPointNote: Steven executes his plan to randomly kill one family member to break the curse, the climactic irreversible act of the film.

scenes.heading: INT. BURGER KING - DAY
scenes.summary: Steven sits at Burger King with Anna and Kim when Martin walks in limping and stares at them; Kim eats her chips hungrily and walks out normally with her parents.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kim's healthy state reveals she survived the shooting, indicating Bob was killed; the curse is broken and the family continues with their loss.
